lesson_meta.title: Patriotism and Duty
lesson_meta.subject: English
lesson_meta.description: Comprehensive lesson notes and quiz questions for 5th standard or equivalent syllabus.

content.text: "Patriotism and Duty" - This lesson emphasizes that true patriotism is not just joining the army and fighting, but also doing one's duty properly as a citizen in society, and showing love and respect to fellow human beings.
Soldier: A person who serves in an army.
Battle: A fight between armies.
Weapon: An object used to attack.
Mahavir Chakra: The second highest military reward in India.
Amar Jawan Jyoti: A memorial for Indian soldiers built after the 1971 Indo-Pak war.
Honour: Great respect.
Sacrifice: Giving up something valued for the sake of something else.
Battalion: A military unit consisting of 300 to 800 soldiers.
Brass bust: A brass statue of the upper part of the human figure.

sections.title: 2. Prose: The Guardians of the Nation

content.text: Armed Forces Flag Day: Observed every year on December 7.
Dr. Karmugilan: A young doctor who studied medicine in the USA and returned to India to provide free medical treatment to village people.
Disease Outbreak: Dengue fever spread in the villages. Karmugilan, who treated the people, was also infected by dengue and died.
Memorial: The people built a hospital in his memory and are providing free medical treatment.
Important for the Spoken English & Grammar section in competitive exams.
Rule 1 (Compound Words): If it is a compound word, split after the first word. (e.g., Bull/dog, Sun/set, Sky/blue, Watch/man).
Rule 2 (Long Vowel Team): If long vowels like (ai, ea, oa, ee, ay, oe) appear, split following them. (e.g., Pea/cock, rea/son, pea/nut).
Rule 3 (r - preceded by vowel): If a vowel (a, e, i, o, u) comes before the letter 'r', split after 'r'. (e.g., Tur/key, pur/ple, bur/ger).
We should treat everyone equally, crossing our inner boundaries.
We should not destroy our integrity in diversity.
Rhyming Words: alike – dislike, in – foreign, proud – crowd.

sections.title: 5. Grammar: Prepositions of Time (in, on, at)

content.text: ON: Days (on Friday), Dates (on 3rd November, on 15th August).
IN: Months (in September), Seasons (in summer), Years (in 1979, in 2013), Parts of the day (in the morning/afternoon/evening).
AT: Clock time (at 7 o'clock, at 4.30 PM), Night time (at night), Meal times (at lunchtime, at dinner).
Jaswant Singh Rawat: A place called 'Jaswantgarh' in Arunachal Pradesh is named after him.
War: Indo-China war 1962.
Battle of Nauranang: Started at 5 AM on November 17, 1962, and lasted for 72 hours (3 days).
Jaswant's Bravery: He fought alone, tricking the Chinese army for 3 days.
Helpers: Two local tribal women named Sela and Nura.
Casualties: Jaswant and the women together killed 300 Chinese soldiers. The truth was known to the Chinese when the person who supplied food was caught.
Martyrdom: To avoid being captured by the Chinese, Jaswant shot himself. Sela died in a bomb blast. Nura was captured alive.
Honour: The Chinese commander returned Jaswant's brass bust. The Indian government awarded him the 'Mahavir Chakra'.
Special Information: The Indian Army still considers him a serving officer and gives him promotions.

sections.title: 7. Let's Know More / Reading Section (National Emblem)

content.text: India's National Emblem is taken from Ashoka's pillar at Sarnath.
It has four lions (but only 3 lions are visible at a time).
At the center is the Dharma chakra, with a Bull on the right and a Horse on the left. All these sit on a Lotus.
The slogan 'Sathyameva Jayate' is written below in Devanagari script.
Its meaning: "Truth alone Triumphs".
I. Choose the Best Answer
1. Where did Karmugilan go for higher studies? Answer: c) USA
2. Which disease spread in the nearby villages? Answer: c) Dengue
3. He got ________ the disease. Answer: a) infected by
4. What did the village people build in his memory? Answer: d) Hospital
II. Fill in the blanks
1. The food they ate was: chickpea sundal
2. The memorial for the soldiers who died for the country: Amar Jawan Jyoti
3. Armed Forces Flag Day: 7th December
4. A person who dies for the country: Soldier
5. A young and talented doctor: Karmugilan
III. Grammar - Prepositions (in, on, at)
1. The summer vacation ends on Monday.
2. I will meet you at 7.30.
3. I don’t drive at night.
4. My birthday falls in September.
5. Birds migrate in spring and autumn.
6. Her birthday is on 6th April.
IV. Jaswantgarh Supplementary Questions (Fill in the blanks & True/False)
1. The place Jaswantgarh is named after the Indian soldier Jaswant Singh Rawat.
2. The lonely Indian post attacked by Chinese troops: Nauranang.
3. The tribal women who helped Jaswant: Two (Sela and Nura).
4. The number of Chinese soldiers killed by Jaswant: 300.
5. The award given to Jaswant Singh Rawat: Mahavir Chakra.
True / False
1. Nauranang is in Himachal Pradesh - False (It is in Arunachal Pradesh)
2. The Battle of Nauranang lasted for three days - True (72 hours)
3. Jaswant decided to stay in his post and fight - True
4. Jaswant was captured alive - False (He shot himself)
5. According to the Indian Army, Jaswant is still a serving officer - True

quiz.question: 1. What is the core theme of this lesson?
quiz.explanation: The lesson emphasizes that patriotism is not just serving in the army, but also doing one's duty properly as a citizen.

quiz.question: 2. What does true patriotism refer to among the following?
options: Doing one's duty in society and showing love
quiz.explanation: True patriotism means doing one's duty properly in society and showing love and respect to fellow human beings.

quiz.question: 3. What is the meaning of the word 'Soldier'?
options: A person who serves in the army
quiz.explanation: A soldier is a person who serves in an army.

quiz.question: 4. What is a fight between armies called?
quiz.explanation: A 'Battle' is a fight between armies.

quiz.question: 5. What is the meaning of the word 'Weapon'?
quiz.explanation: A weapon is an object or tool used to attack.

quiz.question: 6. Which is the second highest military award in India?
quiz.explanation: The Mahavir Chakra is the second highest military reward in India.

quiz.question: 7. The 'Amar Jawan Jyoti' was built after which war?
quiz.explanation: It was built in memory of the Indian soldiers who died in the 1971 Indo-Pak war.

quiz.question: 8. What is the exact meaning of the word 'Honour'?
quiz.explanation: Honour means having or showing great respect.

quiz.question: 9. What is the meaning of the word 'Sacrifice'?
quiz.explanation: Sacrifice means giving up something valued for the sake of something else.

quiz.question: 10. What is the name of a military unit consisting of 300 to 800 soldiers?
quiz.explanation: A Battalion is a military unit consisting of 300 to 800 soldiers.

quiz.question: 11. What is a 'Brass bust'?
options: A brass statue of the upper part of the human figure
quiz.explanation: A brass bust refers to a statue made of brass showing the head, shoulders, and chest of a person.

quiz.question: 12. When is the Armed Forces Flag Day observed?
quiz.explanation: The Armed Forces Flag Day is observed every year on December 7.

quiz.question: 13. Where did Dr. Karmugilan go for his higher studies (medicine)?
quiz.explanation: He studied medicine in the USA and returned to India.

quiz.question: 14. To whom did Dr. Karmugilan provide free medical treatment?
quiz.explanation: He returned to India and provided free medical treatment to village people.

quiz.question: 15. Which disease spread in the villages where Karmugilan worked?
quiz.explanation: Dengue fever spread in the villages.

quiz.question: 16. Karmugilan was __________. (He got ________ the disease)
quiz.explanation: Karmugilan, who treated the people, was also infected by dengue and died.

quiz.question: 17. What did the village people build in memory of Karmugilan?
quiz.explanation: The people built a hospital in his memory and are providing free treatment.

quiz.question: 18. Where should compound words be split during syllabification?
quiz.explanation: For compound words, we should split them after the first word.

quiz.question: 19. What is the correct way to split the word 'Bulldog'?
quiz.explanation: Since Bulldog is a compound word, it is split as Bull/dog.

quiz.question: 20. Where should a word be split if it has a Long Vowel Team (ai, ea, ee, etc.)?
options: At the end of the word
quiz.explanation: If long vowels appear, the word should be split following them.

quiz.question: 21. What is the correct way to split the word 'Peacock'?
quiz.explanation: Because 'ea' is a long vowel team, we split it following the vowels as Pea/cock.

quiz.question: 22. Where should a word be split if a vowel (a,e,i,o,u) precedes 'r'?
quiz.explanation: If a vowel comes before the letter 'r', the split should happen after 'r'.

quiz.question: 23. What is the correct way to split the word 'Turkey'?
quiz.explanation: The vowel 'u' precedes 'r', so it is split after 'r' as Tur/key.

quiz.question: 24. According to the poem 'Patriotism', what should we cross to treat everyone equally?
quiz.explanation: The poem says we should treat everyone equally by crossing our inner boundaries.

quiz.question: 25. What should we not destroy in our diversity, according to the poem?
quiz.explanation: We should not destroy our integrity in diversity.

quiz.question: 26. What is the correct rhyming word for 'alike'?
quiz.explanation: According to the poem, the rhyming word for alike is dislike.

quiz.question: 27. What is the correct rhyming word for 'proud'?
quiz.explanation: According to the poem, the rhyming word for proud is crowd.

quiz.question: 28. Which preposition of time is used before days of the week?
quiz.explanation: The preposition 'ON' is used before days and dates (e.g., on Friday).

quiz.question: 29. Which preposition is used before months and years?
quiz.explanation: The preposition 'IN' is used before months, seasons, and years (e.g., in September, in 1979).

quiz.question: 30. Which preposition is used to indicate clock time and night time?
quiz.explanation: The preposition 'AT' is used for clock time and night time (e.g., at 7 o'clock, at night).

quiz.question: 31. The summer vacation ends _____ Monday. Choose the correct answer.
quiz.explanation: Monday is a day, so 'on' is the correct preposition.

quiz.question: 32. I will meet you _____ 7.30. Choose the correct answer.
quiz.explanation: 7.30 is a specific clock time, so 'at' is used.

quiz.question: 33. I don’t drive _____ night. Choose the correct answer.
quiz.explanation: To refer to the night time, 'at night' is the correct usage.

quiz.question: 34. My birthday falls _____ September. Choose the correct answer.
quiz.explanation: September is a month, so 'in' is the correct preposition.

quiz.question: 35. Birds migrate _____ spring and autumn. Choose the correct answer.
quiz.explanation: For seasons (spring, autumn), the preposition 'in' is used.

quiz.question: 36. Her birthday is _____ 6th April. Choose the correct answer.
quiz.explanation: 6th April is a specific date, so 'on' is the correct preposition.

quiz.question: 37. The place 'Jaswantgarh' is named in memory of whom?
quiz.explanation: The place is named after the Indian soldier Jaswant Singh Rawat.

quiz.question: 38. In which state is Jaswantgarh located?
quiz.explanation: Jaswantgarh is located in Arunachal Pradesh.

quiz.question: 39. In which year did the Indo-China war take place?
quiz.explanation: The Indo-China war took place in the year 1962.

quiz.question: 40. How many continuous hours did the Battle of Nauranang last?
quiz.explanation: The battle started on November 17, 1962, and lasted for 72 hours.

quiz.question: 41. Who was the soldier who fought alone, tricking the Chinese army for 3 days?
quiz.explanation: Jaswant Singh Rawat fought alone, tricking the Chinese army for 3 days.

quiz.question: 42. How many local tribal women helped Jaswant?
quiz.explanation: Two local tribal women named Sela and Nura helped him.

quiz.question: 43. How many Chinese soldiers did Jaswant and the women kill together?
quiz.explanation: Together, they killed 300 Chinese soldiers.

quiz.question: 44. What did Jaswant decide to do to avoid being captured alive by the Chinese?
quiz.explanation: To avoid being captured by the Chinese, Jaswant shot himself.

quiz.question: 45. How does the Indian Army still consider Jaswant Singh Rawat today?
quiz.explanation: The Indian Army still considers him a serving officer and gives him promotions.

quiz.question: 46. Where is India's National Emblem taken from?
quiz.explanation: The National Emblem is taken from Ashoka's pillar at Sarnath.

quiz.question: 47. How many lions are there in the National Emblem of India?
quiz.explanation: It has four lions, but only 3 lions are visible at a time.

quiz.question: 48. Which animals are on the right and left sides of the Dharma Chakra in the National Emblem?
options: Elephant on right, Lion on left
Bull on right, Horse on left
Deer on right, Tiger on left
Horse on right, Bull on left
quiz.explanation: There is a Bull on the right and a Horse on the left of the Dharma Chakra.

quiz.question: 49. In which script is the phrase 'Sathyameva Jayate' written?
quiz.explanation: The phrase 'Sathyameva Jayate' is written below in Devanagari script.

quiz.question: 50. What is the meaning of 'Sathyameva Jayate'?
quiz.explanation: Its meaning is "Truth alone Triumphs".
